faqs.question: How do I know if I have a clothes moth infestation in my Croton-on-Hudson home?
faqs.answer: Signs of clothes moth infestation include small irregular holes in wool clothing, carpets, or upholstery, silken tubes or cases in closets, webbing in fabric corners, and adult moths flying near wardrobes. You may also notice larvae (small cream-colored caterpillars) on affected items or shed larval casings in storage areas.

faqs.question: What is the cost of clothes moth control in Croton-on-Hudson?
faqs.answer: Clothes moth control services in Croton-on-Hudson typically range from $550 to $5,000, depending on the severity of the infestation, the size of your home, and the number of treatments required. Minor infestations in single rooms cost less, while whole-home treatments for severe or recurring problems require more extensive service. We provide free inspections and detailed quotes.

faqs.question: How long does clothes moth treatment take to work?
faqs.answer: Initial treatment typically shows results within 1-2 weeks, but complete elimination may require 4-8 weeks due to the moth life cycle. Multiple treatments are often necessary to target eggs, larvae, and adult moths at different developmental stages. We recommend follow-up inspections to ensure complete eradication and provide prevention guidance.

faqs.question: Can I prevent clothes moths from returning after treatment?
faqs.answer: Yes! Prevention includes regularly cleaning and vacuuming closets, storing seasonal clothing in airtight containers, using cedar blocks or lavender sachets as natural deterrents, and professionally cleaning woolens before storage. We also offer ongoing monitoring and maintenance plans to protect your Croton-on-Hudson home from re-infestation year-round.
